frameworks.name: non-invasive brain stimulation
frameworks.one_line: comparing excitatory NIBS protocols for modulating motor cortex excitability

frameworks.name: cortical excitability modulation
frameworks.one_line: framework that NIBS protocols up-regulate corticospinal and intracortical excitability

frameworks.name: responder analysis
frameworks.one_line: individual-difference framing of stimulation efficacy as response classification

core_question: Do PAS, anodal tDCS, and 20-Hz tACS differ in their excitatory aftereffects on human primary motor cortex compared with Sham?
key_assumptions: TMS-evoked responses index corticospinal and intracortical excitability
Sham provides a valid baseline for response classification
manipulations: stimulation protocol (PAS, anodal tDCS, 20-Hz tACS, Sham)
key_findings: none of the three protocols significantly modulated corticospinal or intracortical excitability versus Sham
aftereffects were highly heterogeneous and variable across individuals
interpretations: high inter-individual variability may explain weak group-level effects of excitatory NIBS
responder/non-responder heterogeneity challenges uniform efficacy of these protocols
limitations_noted: highly heterogeneous and variable individual aftereffects
